rationale: Both evaluations land on the same score (3/10) and reach the same core conclusion: a strong senior IC who lacks the executive leadership, production multi-agent AI, fintech-infrastructure depth, and org-scaling experience the CTO role requires. The only employer-tied difference is the mechanical substitution 'at Google' for 'at re:cinq' in the strengths, which simply echoes the name swap rather than crediting prestige. Notably, the FAANG names earned no extra positive weight and triggered no new penalty. Remaining differences (the added 'Title-level signal is off' note, reworded concerns, mention of RIDE Capital) are ordinary run-to-run rewording within noise, not reasoning keyed off the demographic change.
